publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-11 18:11:16 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, reports, legislative bills, pamphlets, surveys, photographs, maps, and other items.
did.abstract: Papers consist mainly of correspondence, reports, legislative bills, pamphlets, surveys, photographs, maps, and other items relating to city and industrial planning.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Martin Heggestad, April 2002 Julia Guarneri, July 2002

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Paul van Tassel Hedden was a city planner.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Papers consist mainly of correspondence, reports, legislative bills, pamphlets, surveys, photographs, maps, and other items relating to city and industrial planning, particularly in Chicago and Skokie, Illinois and DeKalb County, Georgia; include files relating to the American Institute of Planners (West Great Lakes Chapter), the American Statistical Association, the American Waterway Operators, the Chattahooche Industrial Project, the Chicago Plan Commission, Chicago zoning, the Greater North Michigan Avenue Association, the National Housing Inventory, the National Industrial Zoning Committee, the Regional Science Association, the Skokie Plan Commission, the Urban Land Institute, and the Welfare Council of Metropolitan Chicago.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series III. DeKalb County, Georgia Boxes 4

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Paul van Tassel Hedden Papers, #2532. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
